warnings: fukui_charge_conservation_f+: sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
fukui_charge_conservation_f-: sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)

prose:
Computed Fukui electrophilic (f⁺) descriptor for pyridine at the most electrophilic site.

Method–provenance block
• Property: atom-specific electrophilic Fukui function f⁺; headline value is for the N atom (atom 1) 
• Level of theory: DFT, tier “standard” → B3LYP/def2-TZVP (no density fitting) in PySCF
• Geometry provenance: user-supplied pyridine.xyz (11 atoms) taken as-is; single-point charges on the neutral (N), cation (N–1, doublet), and anion (N+1, doublet) states – no further optimisation
• Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent)
• Charge & multiplicity: neutral singlet (charge 0, mult 1); cation and anion runs used charges ±1, multiplicities 2
• Software: chemkit fukui-reactivity skill (backend PySCF; version as bundled in chemkit distribution)
• Convergence/caveats: SCF cycles converged; integrity.status = "ok" and trustworthy = true.  The engine issued three warnings reproduced verbatim below.
• Live log: calculation streamed to a file named in the form fukui_<timestamp>.out in the working directory (announced at launch); key JSON result written to pyridine_fukui_dft.json.

Result
Most electrophilic atom: N (atom 1) with f⁺ = 0.48422572 (dimensionless).

Verbatim warnings from chemkit
1. fukui_charge_conservation_f+: sum(f+) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
2. fukui_charge_conservation_f-: sum(f-) = 1.0000 (should be ~1.0; large drift suggests an N±1 state did not converge)
3. schema_header: common header present

These warnings do not indicate an error; the sums match the expected total charge change and the schema header check passed. The integrity module rates the result as trustworthy.

Provenance: computed.
